entries.d: a vehicle that travels through space
entries.s: The _____ flew past the moon and into deep darkness.

entries.d: The person in charge who tells others what to do
entries.s: The military _____ of the ship told all the sailors to get ready for the mission.

entries.d: Special clothes you wear to survive in space
entries.s: You need a _____ so you can breathe outside the ship.

entries.d: A creature that comes from another world
entries.s: In the film, the _____ was green and had big eyes.

entries.d: A strange flying thing in the sky that nobody can explain
entries.s: Some people say they saw a _____ over the city last night.

entries.d: A building where people plan trips to the sky and other worlds
entries.s: We visited the _____ and saw real rockets.

entries.d: A big round thing in the sky that goes around a star
entries.s: Earth is the _____ where we all live.

entries.d: The person who is in charge at work
entries.s: My mum's _____ at the office called her about a meeting tomorrow morning.

entries.d: A thick wire used to connect things
entries.s: The TV does not work because the power _____ that connects it to the wall is broken.

entries.d: The most important city of a country
entries.s: London is the _____ of England. It is where the king and the government are.

entries.d: To join two things together
entries.s: Can you _____ your phone to the speaker so we can play music?

entries.d: A brave person who helps or saves other people
entries.s: The firefighter was a real _____ and saved the cat.

entries.d: Something built to do a job, like a washing or coffee maker
entries.s: This kitchen _____ makes fresh orange juice in ten seconds when you press the button.

entries.d: To fix something that is broken
entries.s: My dad _____ my bike after the wheel came off, using his tools in the garage.

entries.d: Everything outside our world, where the stars are
entries.s: One day I want to travel into _____.

entries.d: A figure of a person or animal made from stone or metal
entries.s: There is a big bronze _____ of a horse and its rider in the town centre, made by a famous artist 100 years ago.

entries.d: To get control of something
entries.s: In the story, robots _____ the whole city.

entries.d: A person who goes from place to place
entries.s: The _____ going around the world had a big backpack, a map, and a passport with many stamps.

entries.d: A person who comes to see a place or a person
entries.s: Our school had a special _____ from England today who came to give us a talk about London.

entries.d: A small metal thing you use to open a lock
entries.s: I forgot my _____ and couldn't get into the house.

entries.d: The group of people who work on a ship or plane
entries.s: The _____ on the plane was very friendly — the pilot and all five flight attendants.

entries.d: A big vehicle with wings that flies through the sky
entries.s: The _____ flew over our house and made a loud noise.

entries.d: A person who knows a lot about something
entries.s: She is an _____ on African animals — she has studied them for 20 years and wrote three books about them.

entries.d: A trick that makes people believe something that is not true
entries.s: The story about the monster was just a _____.

entries.d: When people try to find out what really happened
entries.s: The police started an _____ to find the missing painting. They looked for clues everywhere.

entries.d: A picture taken with a camera
entries.s: She showed me an old black-and-white _____ of her grandparents taken in 1960.

entries.d: To break something so badly it cannot be fixed
entries.s: The strong storm completely _____ many trees in the park last night. Nothing was left.

entries.d: To take a person away by force
entries.s: In the film, bad people _____ the prince and held him in a secret castle for a week.

entries.d: Something silly that does not make sense
entries.s: Don't talk _____! Of course the Earth is round — everyone knows that.

entries.d: Nice to sit or lie on, making you feel good
entries.s: This soft sofa is very _____. I could sit here all day without getting a sore back.

entries.d: Words you say to someone who is too excited or scared
entries.s: _____! Stop shouting and breathe slowly. Everything is going to be fine.

entries.d: If the situation is like that, then
entries.s: You don't like fish? _____, let's order chicken.
